2026-04-10T00:26:45.0842194Z state without impacting any branches by switching back to a branch.
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0842488Z 
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0842670Z If you want to create a new branch to retain commits you create, you may
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0843120Z do so (now or later) by using -c with the switch command. Example:
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0843380Z 
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0843478Z   git switch -c <new-branch-name>
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0843654Z

2026-04-10T00:26:45.0843908Z 
2026-04-10T00:26:45.0843990Z   git switch -

2026-04-10T00:26:54.7484305Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-10T00:26:54.7484697Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-10T00:26:54.7485034Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-10T00:26:54.7485296Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-10T00:36:01.3966708Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js && git diff origin/main...HEAD -- sdk/ai/ai-projects/review/ai-projects-node.api.md 2>&1 | grep -A3 "BetaInsightsOperations" | head -30
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3967984Z 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3968290Z ◆ Now I can see the full picture of breaking changes:
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3968748Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3970880Z   1. **`BetaEvaluatorsListLatestVersionsOptionalParams` renamed to `BetaEvaluatorsListOptionalParams`** - This is in the GA (2.0.2) as `BetaEvaluatorsListLatestVersionsOptionalParams` → removed and renamed to `BetaEvaluatorsListOptionalParams`. This breaks anyone using the old type.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3972836Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3977419Z   2. **`BetaInsightsOperations.get` parameter renamed** - `id: string` → `insightId: string`. This is a parameter rename in a method signature in an interface. Since it's an interface definition in TypeScript, users who implemented this interface (writing a mock) would have to update. But users CALLING the method would not be affected (positional arguments). However, for named/options-based calls in some patterns... Actually in TypeScript, renaming a parameter in an interface or type definition doesn't break callers—only implementors. And since this is an operations interface, it's unlikely users are implementing it. Let me note it as a low concern.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3980999Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3981442Z   3. **`FoundryFeaturesOptInKeys` removed** - This was exported in GA 2.0.2. Removing it is a breaking change.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3982075Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3982507Z   4. **`TextResponseFormatConfiguration` and related types removed** - All exported in GA 2.0.2. Major breaking change.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3983010Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3983350Z   5. **`Tool` interface removed** - This was exported in GA 2.0.2. Removing it is a breaking change.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3983751Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3984560Z   6. **`ToolChoiceParamType`, `ToolChoiceParamUnion`, `ToolType`, `ToolUnion` removed** - All of these tool-related types were exported in GA 2.0.2, so their removal is a significant breaking change for anyone relying on these type definitions.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3985436Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3986557Z   7. **`AgentDefinitionOptInKeys` values changed** - Looking at the diff more carefully, the type definition is being replaced entirely. The old version only included `HostedAgents=V1Preview` and `WorkflowAgents=V1Preview`, but the new one adds `ContainerAgents=V1Preview` and `AgentEndpoints=V1Preview` to the union.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3987973Z

2026-04-10T00:36:01.3990445Z 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3991128Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js && git show "@azure/ai-projects_2.0.2:sdk/ai/ai-projects/review/ai-projects-node.api.md" 2>&1 | grep -B5 "container_protocol_versions"
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3991789Z 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3992519Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js && git diff origin/main...HEAD -- sdk/ai/ai-projects/review/ai-projects-node.api.md 2>&1 | grep -B5 "code_text\|container_protocol_versions" | head -30
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3993227Z 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3993508Z ◆ Now I'm getting more clarity. Let me analyze the breaking changes more carefully:
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3993882Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3996052Z   1. **`CodeBasedEvaluatorDefinition.code_text`** changed from required (`code_text: string`) to optional (`code_text?: string`). Making a required property optional is generally NOT a breaking change for callers (they can still pass it). But for code that implemented this interface and expected `code_text` to always be defined, it might need updating. Actually, making a required field optional is NOT a breaking change for code that USES the type (since they were already required to provide it). For code that READS the type, it's technically more restrictive (now needs undefined check). Let me assess: this is a **minor concern** rather than a hard breaking change.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3998734Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3999105Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.3999584Z   2. **`HostedAgentDefinition.container_protocol_versions`** has the same shift from required to optional, so same analysis applies.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4000126Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4001134Z   Now I'm seeing the real breaking changes: **`FoundryFeaturesOptInKeys`** was exported in GA 2.0.2 but appears to be gone, and **`TextResponseFormatConfiguration`** along with related types have been removed entirely. The **`Tool`** interface and its associated types are also missing, which is significant.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4002216Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4003248Z   There's also a rename from **`BetaEvaluatorsListLatestVersionsOptionalParams`** to **`BetaEvaluatorsListOptionalParams`** that breaks compatibility. I need to figure out whether these removed types still exist under different names or if they've been consolidated into the openai package itself.

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4007720Z

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4009413Z

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4009773Z

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4012147Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4012293Z

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4017563Z 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4018324Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js && cat sdk/ai/ai-projects/review/ai-projects-node.api.md | grep -E "^export (interface|type) (Tool\b|ToolType|ToolUnion|ToolChoiceParam)" | head -20
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4019060Z 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4020675Z ◆ Now I see the full picture. The `Tool`, `ToolType`, `ToolChoiceParamType`, `ToolChoiceParamUnion`, `ToolUnion` types still exist, but with modified values. The diff showed them as removed because their values changed (e.g., `ToolType` now includes `"computer"`, `"namespace"`, `"tool_search"`, `"work_iq_preview"` which were not in the GA version, but the old values are still present). These are additive additions to union types, not removals.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4022176Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4022975Z   For `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` - the GA version had `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` as the base interface, and the new API renames/replaces it with `TextResponseFormat`. 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4023685Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4024938Z   Looking at the specific type mappings, I can see that `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` got renamed to `TextResponseFormat`, while the related types like `TextResponseFormatConfigurationResponseFormatText` became `TextResponseFormatText`, and `TextResponseFormatConfigurationType` actually persists in both versions despite the base type rename.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4026254Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4027488Z   Then there's `BetaEvaluatorsListLatestVersionsOptionalParams` being shortened to `BetaEvaluatorsListOptionalParams` — this is a breaking change even though it's a Beta-prefixed interface that was exported in GA.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4028460Z

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4033417Z ◆ Now I have the full picture. Let me compile the list of breaking changes:
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4033759Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4034008Z   **Breaking Changes (GA-stable items that were removed/renamed):**
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4034323Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4035124Z   1. **`TextResponseFormatConfiguration` → renamed to `TextResponseFormat`**: The interface was renamed. Users referencing `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` type will get compilation errors.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4035873Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4036385Z   2. **`TextResponseFormatConfigurationResponseFormatText` → renamed to `TextResponseFormatText`**: Breaking rename.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4036895Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4037703Z   3. **`TextResponseFormatConfigurationResponseFormatJsonObject` → renamed to `TextResponseFormatJsonObject`**: Breaking rename.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4038266Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4038721Z   4. **`TextResponseFormatConfigurationUnion` → renamed to `TextResponseFormatUnion`**: Breaking rename. 
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4039181Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4039735Z   5. **`format` property type change**: The `format` property in some types shifted from `TextResponseFormatConfigurationUnion` to `TextResponseFormatUnion`.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4040355Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4040896Z   6. **`BetaEvaluatorsListLatestVersionsOptionalParams` → renamed to `BetaEvaluatorsListOptionalParams`**: Breaking rename.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4041438Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4042299Z   7. **`CodeBasedEvaluatorDefinition.code_text`**: Changed from required `string` to optional `string?`. While technically source-compatible for object literals, this could create subtle behavioral issues if callers relied on it always being defined.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4043221Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4044496Z   Actually, I need to reconsider the impact here. Making a required property optional is more permissive for callers passing objects, but it breaks code that destructures or implements the interface expecting the property to always exist. For implementations specifically, this widens the interface, which could cause issues if code assumed `code_text` would always be provided.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4045824Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4047295Z   Looking at the version history, I see `container_protocol_versions` underwent the same change from required to optional. Given these breaking changes across multiple properties, the current version bump to `2.1.0` seems insufficient—this should really be a major version bump to `3.0.0`.
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4048515Z   
2026-04-10T00:36:01.4049259Z   But there's another issue: the type renames like `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` → `TextResponseFormat` remove the old e... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2420 chars]

2026-04-10T00:36:01.4156598Z

This is inconsistent with the established pattern in this package and is a new naming issue introduced by this PR.

Use this for inline code review feedback, suggestions, or questions about specific code changes. For general PR comments not tied to specific lines, use add_comment instead. When the workflow is configured with `target: \"*\"`, you must specify `pull_request_number` to indicate which PR to target. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 10 review comment(s) can be created. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Review comment content in Markdown. Provide specific, actionable feedback about the code at this location.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"line":{"description":"Line number for the comment. For single-line comments, this is the target line. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line.","type":["number","string"]},"path":{"description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). Must be a file that was changed in the PR.","type":"string"},"pull_request_number":{"description":"Pull request number to add the review comment to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 876 in github.com/owner/repo/pull/876). If omitted, adds the comment to the PR that triggered this workflow. Required when the workflow target is '*' (any PR) — omitting it will cause the comment to fail.","type":["number","string"]},"repo":{"description":"Target repository in 'owner/repo' format. If you don't call this tool, review comments are still submitted as a COMMENT review. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 review(s) can be submitted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Overall review summary in Markdown. Defaults to COMMENT when omitted.","enum":["APPROVE","REQUEST_CHANGES","COMMENT"],"type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"type":"object"},"name":"submit_pull_request_review"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running.
